faq.question: Why does the plugin fail with `ReferenceError: ReadableStream is not defined`?
faq.answer:
This error occurs when the plugin is run on an unsupported or outdated version of Node.js. The `homebridge-homeconnect` plugin requires **Node.js 18.x or 20.x (LTS)** to function correctly.

The `ReadableStream` object was only exposed on the global object starting with Node.js 18.0.0. Attempting to run the plugin on older versions, such as Node.js 16, will result in this crash during the loading process. This version requirement ensures compliance with Homebridge Verified plugin standards.

To resolve this issue:
1. Upgrade your Node.js installation to the latest 18.x or 20.x LTS release.
2. If you are using HOOBS, ensure your system is updated to a version that includes a supported Node.js runtime. Note that as this is a Homebridge plugin, any platform-specific issues with HOOBS should be referred to HOOBS support.
faq.semantic_abstract: The `homebridge-homeconnect` plugin requires Node.js 18.x or 20.x LTS to support global `ReadableStream` functionality and comply with Homebridge Verified plugin standards. Version-specific runtime errors such as `ReferenceError: ReadableStream is not defined` occur when the plugin is executed on Node.js 16 or earlier, a common configuration in legacy HOOBS environments.
